# Treasure Island

Robert Louis Stevenson's adventure supports lessons in courage, greed, loyalty, suspense, and first-person narration.

## Spelling Words

| Core Words | Challenge Words |
|---|---|
| island | adventure |
| sailor | mutiny |
| ship | treasure |
| cabin | voyage |
| map | courage |
| chest | suspicion |
| pirate | dangerous |
| captain | loyalty |
| sword | cowardice |
| secret | wilderness |

## Word Study

- Sort nautical words from character-trait words.
- Keep a running list of words connected to danger.
- Write sentences using three map words: north, coast, and harbor.

## Narration

- Retell a chapter from Jim Hawkins's point of view.
- Identify a moment when a character must choose whom to trust.
- Explain how Stevenson creates suspense before danger appears.

## Writing Prompts

- Write a ship log for one day of the voyage.
- Describe a hidden place without naming what is hidden there.
- Argue whether courage means feeling no fear or acting despite fear.

## Projects

- Draw a treasure map with a key, compass rose, and written directions.
- Make a character loyalty chart that changes as the story develops.
- Present a short report on sailing terms used in the novel.
